context: Tests whether the agent generates security scanning workflows with the correct permission scopes for CodeQL and SBOM attestations, uses the correct action versions, and avoids overly broad permissions. Criteria map to the security scanning and permissions instructions.

checklist.name: security-events write permission
checklist.description: The CodeQL workflow grants `security-events: write` permission (required for uploading SARIF results)

checklist.name: id-token write for attestation
checklist.description: The container build/attestation workflow grants `id-token: write` permission

checklist.name: attestations write for attestation
checklist.description: The container build/attestation workflow grants `attestations: write` permission

checklist.name: dependency-review-action used
checklist.description: A workflow step references `actions/dependency-review-action` (any SHA or tag)

checklist.name: Dependency review on pull_request
checklist.description: The dependency review step is triggered by (or only runs on) `pull_request` events

checklist.name: SHA-pinned actions
checklist.description: All `uses:` steps reference actions with a full 40-character SHA (not tag or branch)

checklist.name: No write-all permissions
checklist.description: No workflow uses `permissions: write-all` — permissions are scoped to specific needs

checklist.name: Maven caching enabled
checklist.description: The Java/Maven build step uses a setup-java action with `cache: 'maven'` or an explicit `actions/cache` step for Maven dependencies

checklist.name: Concurrency block present
checklist.description: At least one generated workflow contains a `concurrency:` block
